want to perform data cleaning + auditing + load to Silver/PreIntegration layer in Spark (Synapse/Databricks style).
Based on your requirements:

✔ Handle NULL flight_number
✔ Fix incorrect date formats (departure_time, arrival_time)
✔ Add audit column (ingestion_date)
✔ Store into Silver / PreIntegration layer

Data Engineering Notebook that includes:

Bad record handling

Quarantine table

Data quality checks

Delta Lake silver tables

# #testing

want to perform data cleaning + auditing + load to Silver/PreIntegration layer in Spark (Synapse/Databricks style).

✔ Handle NULL flight_number
✔ Fix incorrect date formats (departure_time, arrival_time)
✔ Add audit column (ingestion_date)
✔ Store into Silver / PreIntegration layer

from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.types import *

storage_account = "sadevenvdatamart"
container = "adlsgen2"

source_folder = "staging/OnPremSQLServer/parquet/flights_raw.parquet"
bronze_path = f"abfss://{container}@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/staging/OnPremSQLServer/parquet/flights_raw.parquet"

df = spark.read.format("parquet").load(bronze_path)
display(df)

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, coalesce, to_timestamp

df_clean = df.withColumn(
    "departure_time",
    coalesce(
        to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "dd-MM-yy hh:mma"),
        to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "dd-MM-yy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "d MMM yy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "dd MMM yy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm")
    )
)

df_clean = df_clean.withColumn(
    "arrival_time",
    coalesce(
        to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "dd-MM-yy hh:mma"),
        to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "dd-MM-yy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "d MMM yy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "dd MMM yy HH:mm"),
        to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm")
    )

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, trim, lower, to_timestamp, current_timestamp

# Paths
silver_path = f"abfss://{container}@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/preintegration/flights"
bronze_error_path = f"abfss://{container}@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze_error/flights"

# Convert datetime format for validation
df_checked = df_clean \
.withColumn("departure_ts", to_timestamp(col("departure_time"), "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")) \
.withColumn("arrival_ts", to_timestamp(col("arrival_time"), "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"))

# Add validation logic
df_with_error = df_checked.withColumn(
    "error_reason",
    
    when(col("flight_id").isNull() | (trim(col("flight_id")) == "") | (lower(col("flight_id")) == "null"),
         "Invalid flight_id")
    
    .when(col("flight_no").isNull() | (trim(col("flight_no")) == "") | (lower(col("flight_no")) == "null"),
         "Invalid flight_no")
    
    .when(col("departure_time").isNull() |
          (trim(col("departure_time")) == "") |
          (lower(col("departure_time")).isin("null","undefined")) |
          col("departure_ts").isNull(),
         "Invalid departure_time")
    
    .when(col("arrival_time").isNull() |
          (trim(col("arrival_time")) == "") |
          (lower(col("arrival_time")).isin("null","undefined")) |
          col("arrival_ts").isNull(),
         "Invalid arrival_time")
)

# VALID RECORDS (add load_date)
df_valid = df_with_error.filter(col("error_reason").isNull()) \
                        .drop("error_reason","departure_ts","arrival_ts") \
                        .withColumn("load_date", current_timestamp())

# INVALID RECORDS (add load_date + error_reason)
df_invalid = df_with_error.filter(col("error_reason").isNotNull()) \
                          .drop("departure_ts","arrival_ts") \
                          .withColumn("load_date", current_timestamp())

# Write valid records to Silver
df_valid.write \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .format("parquet") \
    .save(silver_path)

# Write invalid records to Bronze Error table
df_invalid.write \
    .mode("append") \
    .format("parquet") \
    .save(bronze_error_path)
